market.summary: Major indexes closed lower and the Nasdaq slid as oil prices touching $95 and rising Treasury yields exerted broad market pressure. Traders are maintaining a cautious stance ahead of critical after-hours earnings reports from Tesla and Alphabet.
market.bull: Strong individual earnings beats like 3M and positive momentum from AI chip deals (AMD) could spark a rally if Big Tech results top expectations.
market.bear: High energy costs around $95 oil and gaining Treasury yields could keep equity valuations under pressure if mega-cap tech earnings disappoint.
market.watch: After-hours quarterly earnings and guidance from Alphabet and Tesla, alongside oil price and yield movements.

takes.note: WTI crude touching $95 is providing clear tailwinds, boosting ExxonMobil up 1.9% and helping Phillips 66 outpace the broader market.
AMD jumped following new AI chip deals with Cerebras and BofA doubled down on Micron, though Texas Instruments dipped despite beating Q2 estimates.
Stock soared after reporting a second-quarter earnings beat and raising its full-year guidance.
Markets are tense and the Nasdaq slid ahead of post-close reports, which are expected to heavily influence sentiment and peripheral names like Micron.

disclaimer: AI interpretation of real public headlines. Not a forecast, not investment advice — markets can move against any read. Confidence levels reflect genuine uncertainty.
